desc:
The crudely carved tunnel extends in either direction. More attention seems to
have been paid to the floor here as the footing is sure, but it appears to get
more tricky to the west.
name: A Narrow Tunnel

desc:
Here, the cave dips lower into the bowels of the earth, collecting the moisture
from the surrounding walls, and creating a shallow pool. About 6 centimeters
deep, the polished surface is broken by many crooked rocks jutting above the
surface. Littered in every direction are the bones of the small lizards that
make up the staple food for the majority of the invalid orcs. This appears to be
their main hunting ground.
name: A Cave

desc:
This area looks like the unlucky victim of an angry god's wrath. It looks only
vaguely cave-like, as though a mighty fist punched violently upon these rocks,
smashing an irregular and sunken hole into the hardened granite. The slick mossy
walls drip beads of water into tiny pockets along the jagged floor. Combined
with the heavy proliferation of broken rocks, this ground makes an excellent
home for small little creatures. Faint clicking and buzzing can be heard amongst
the rocks, and farther ahead into the darkness.
name: A Cave

desc:
Stale, diseased air permeates your clothing and flares your nostrils. All around
you, bits of bones and tattered cloth lay strewn in utter apathy. Here, in this
cavernous underground, the refugees and unwanted orcs live out their dying days.
Most are old and crippled, of no use to anyone and unable to survive if not for
the abundance of slow, weak prey that inhabit the surrounding rocks. Some are
perhaps younger, but none without ghastly deficiencies, making them miserable,
weak, cowardly beasts.
name: Cavern

desc:
The hard uneven ground is padded here by thickly piled skins. Off to one corner,
a medley of medications and salves lay haphazardly and carelessly stuffed in
broken clay jars. Little comfort however can be taken in the surroundings. The
smell of stale blood and infected flesh cuts sharply through the humid warm air.
This is certainly a place to rest, but for many, their final rest. A broken clay
jar rests in pieces here...
name: A Cave

desc:
Altogether uncomfortable, even by orcs' standards, these pocked walls drip with
a tepid moisture. Unsure if the moisture comes from the rocks, or the crowded
breaths of so many orcs, the smell is quite pungent and stifling. Along the
uneven floor, attempts at comfort have been made: piles of greasy warg fur lay
bundled in heaps, partly negating the rocky surface beneath.
name: A Cave

desc:
Rusted weapons and broken shields lie hanging on pegs, or thrown casually down.
This appears to have once been the main armourery for the forward gates, but is
now deserted and unused. Since new passages were built closer to Wolf's Gate,
the guard's armoury was relocated to a more suitable spot. The worn tunnel is a
relic from a time when this area was constantly used, now, the most frequent
visitors are little rodents scavenging for food.
name: A Cave

desc:
This small cramped cave was obviously built recently. Rough and irregular, the
walls have no shape or pattern. The rock, a light porous limestone, burns at
your skin and urges you to leave. Incredibly, someone or thing has made this a
home; a light bed of pelts rests over most of the cave, barely enough room to
lie asleep in. In the other corner, bones of fish and reptiles, a meager but
forced diet.
name: A Cave

desc:
Rough and jagged, this tunnel is a crudely crafted extension of the natural
fissure in the wall. Obviously not made by the skilled hands which carved the
Burrow, this corridor was built quickly, and with little skill. As though
travelling through a hallway of a sinking ship, this tunnel lists heavily
downwards and pitched to the right. Faint motion and shuffling can be heard
beyond the makeshift partition ahead.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
Over time, the constant beat of thousands of feet have softened and weakend
these walls. Years of constant vibrations have caved in the softer rock, leaving
a natural opening in this wall. The smooth marble-like walls of the Burrow
roughly end here, slaking off into a mass of rubble underfoot. Venturing deeper
inwards will lead you away from the safe footing of the smooth thoroughfare.
name: A Natural Crack

desc:
Made by the same crude labour that built the tunnel to the east, work in this
direction was apparently halted. Whether it was the resolve of the rock to
prevent passage this way, or sheer laziness of the workers, the area lies
unfinished and irregular. Shaped like a pear, the cavern grows thinner and
abruptly stops about 2 meters from where you stand.
name: A Cavern

desc:
You stand within the Burrow, a tube-like tunnel carved through the centre of the
mountain above you. To the east and the west it stretches as if by the force of
its own will, cleaving the mountain stone before it. Your feet step sure-footed
upon the smooth floor of stone, smoother than any cobble or wooden floor. The
walls arch above your head, a dome of grey rock above as the floor is a bowl
before you as you follow the rock of the tunnel into the darkness.
name: The Burrow

desc:
Ever onward this underground road travels, its course shaped eastern and western
by the oppressing rock around it. Carved out of the mountain ore, it winds
through the peak's bowels like a lonely worm, digging its way through the
earth's darkest and hidden places, buried far beneath the fields of Bree or the
evil desolation of Mordor. Here it is you stand, in a pocket of life beneath the
mountain stone, within the embrace of this subterranean highway known as The
Burrow.
name: The Burrow

desc:
Confident and straight in its eastern and western course, The Burrow extends
from where you stand like a snake with two heads, each pulling in opposite
directions, held taught in its course by rocky petrification. The smooth walls
extend their curving embrace on all sides of you, the grey hewn and smooth like
flesh. The bowled flooring slides you towards the centre, where your foot finds
easy progress over stones as smooth as glass.
name: The Burrow Intersection

desc:
Wide and spacious, this tunnel opening appears to be a main branch off the
Burrow. Girded and supported by smooth granite arches, this junction could
withstand the force of a legion of pounding trolls. Although cracked from years
of neglect and stress, they still give you a sense of security, as though
nothing from above could cave in these smooth walls.
name: A Tunnel Mouth

desc:
Arched like the back of a screeching cat, this tunnel angles upwards and then
bows back at a downward slope. Torches line the walls at even intervals
indicating that this is either a somewhat important, or well used, route. Up in
the distance however, the yellow light fails to break the darkness, leaving you
to wonder what lies in the deep pool of black ahead.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel here is wide and smooth. Flames lick at the walls from torches lit
along the sides. Crackling with a hiss of steam, the torches are the only sound
you can hear, magnified and reverberated against the glass like surface. Here,
the tunnel opens a new mouth to the east, and continues further into the
darkness to the south.
name: A Cavern

desc:
The tunnel sharply cuts to the west now, preventing you from seeing what lies
there. Perhaps it is of some importance, as the area around you looks well worn
and kept. A sagging and rotted door lies in your path. Why would those who
maintained this area so well have failed to repair an obviously broken door?
name: A Tunnel

desc:
The natural rock of this cave is made of some type of limestone. Pourous and
fragile, it makes the perfect nesting ground for Slug larvae. The tiny holes
seep and bubble a green slime, pulsing and oozing out of the rock's pores. You
almost have difficulty moving, as your feet congeal to the sticky slug vomit
below.
name: A Cave

desc:
The tunnel smoothens slightly here. The walls and ceiling still bulge and twist
with stubborn wills of their own, but the floor here is smooth and passable.
Faint tracks lay etched in the stone where at one point, ore laden carts once
traversed. Now, filled with rusty-red water, they resemble small arteries
travelling deeper into the mines.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
The walls bleed pasty red drop around you. Streaks of oxidized metal, turned
bright red, resemble violent scars upon these hollowed walls. Far deeper, a
bright streak of silver and copper catches your eyes, sparkling down the length
of the vein, and disappearing into the darkness ahead. A weathered and sagging
sign stands here.
name: A Cave

desc:
The tunnels twist and turn as they lead down into the mines. Stone walls have
been carved here by brute force, and timbers shore up the mine entrance to the
south. From within you can hear the sounds of metal on stone and rock dust hangs
in the air. An abandoned ore cart has been overturned here.
name: Guard Cave

desc:
The tunnel, showing the strain of extreme usage, runs in an easterly direction.
The footing is sure and the walls are smooth. There are no protruding rock
outcroppings or dangling fragments of rock to reach up and snare the unwary
traveller.
name: A Worn Tunnel

desc:
The Burrow continues to make its way east here, eternally pushing aside the
thick stone to tunnel its lonely way through the depths of the mountain. On
either side the tunnel walls rise up like a wave, as if to crash down upon you,
arching up into the ceiling of this underground road. What little your sight can
glean in the distance, the tunnel simply continues away from you, until the
gloom of the dark and oppressive mountain closes around you and limits your
sight.
name: The Burrow

desc:
Once the backwater channels for a mighty subterranean river, these cavern walls
lack the smooth countenance typical of most of these tunnels. Uneven and covered
with dry silt, the slowly moving water never softend and smoothed these pitted
walls. The cavern leads both east and north, to the south lies the Burrow.
name: A Cavern

desc:
Here the secret trail comes to an end. Directly south, under the protection of a
hidden granite goblin door, Wolf's Gate lies. From this vantage point, orcs and
goblins can add a second attack or bypass enemies along the Burrow. Scanning the
cool grey granite around you, you feel relieved that you are on this side of the
wall, an upper hand over any enemy.
name: Goblins End

desc:
Here the Burrow abruptly ends in a wall of stone broken only by a thick pair of
metal studded doors. From underneath the portals, your feet pick up a light
breeze, could this be a way out? The walls are wide and smooth, the floor bowed
in the middle by the passing of many feet. Into the darkness, to the west, The
Burrow begins, spread out before you like the bowels of a mighty snake. Your
sight can only carry so far, the mighty tunnel sliding away before you into the
darkness and subterranean gloom.
name: The Wolf's Gate

desc:
Carved out of the rocky granite, this tiny alcove serves as a guard post,
protecting the Burrow from any intruders. Jagged and hastily made, it can hold a
maximum of three guards at any one time. Further behind it, a large iron gate
rests on rusty hinges. Its purpose, unknown.
name: Guards Post

desc:
Cut from the same smooth granite as most of these tunnels, this deep grey
passage leads along a north-south axis. To the west, an opened cavern reeks with
the smell of dirt and decay, unabled to be contained within its vast openness
the odor belches into this tunnel. Another cavern opens to the east, a growling
noise can be heard coming from there.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
Carrying you firmly along these tunnels, the smooth granite floors carry the
sound of your footsteps down the corridor in front of you. Built rather
recently, this passage lacks the worn smoothness of the main passageways. A set
of iron bars, a makeshift holding cell for enemies caught spying within this
sector, blocks your movement to the south. The tunnel continues along its
natural course with a sharp west-north turn.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
Dozens of ravenous, smelly wargs, locked in a big cage, fill this room with
their blood-chilling growls and howls. This is where the Goblins of Wolf's Gate
train their favourite mounts and where, between one raid and another, they feed
them with raw meat - or with captured travellers, whenever possible. A smaller
cage houses some evil-looking black birds.
name: Warg Kennels

desc:
Surrounded by smooth granite walls, this rock acts as a better barrier than most
of the metals that could otherwise be used. Damp and cold, it offers little
comfort, not even a fur or blanket to rest upon. In fact, this small cubicle is
completly devoid of anything, just four blank walls staring at you. A set of
iron manacles rest here.
name: Dungeon

desc:
The ceiling is very low and sagging wooden beams support a large amount of dirt
and stone above your head. A large spider scurries away at your approach, and to
the south you can see some activity and hear the rattling of chains. Somewhere
in the distance someone is screaming.
name: Mineshaft

desc:
The air is putrid and stale in the pits. From deeper in the mine you can hear
the sound of steel striking rock, and the harsh cries of orkish overseers. Piles
of slag line the corridor, and on one of the rubbish heaps lies the mutilated
corpse of an elf. To the east, the corrider continues deeper into the mines, and
to the north it leads back towards the Burrow.
name: Entrance to Mines

desc:
Carved quickly and without skill, these gnashed and uneven walls drip icy water
from an underground stream nearby. The floor, slippery and wet, forces you to
slow down and tread carefully. A slip in these jagged tunnels would be painful.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
Faint grumbling and wailing can be heard from the make-shift prison ahead.
Having no desire to end up there yourself, it is probably not wise to
investigate. You can simply continue on your way, by heading along the straight
east-west passage.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
The walls bleed pasty red drops around you. Streaks of oxidized metal, turned
bright red, resemble violent scars upon these hollowed walls. Once perhaps, this
area was mined for precious metals. Now left in abandonment, the walls have
turned red with rust, giving an eerie and life-like appearence to this crumbling
tunnel. A weathered and sagging sign stands here.
name: A Cave

desc:
Digging your heels into the broken rock floor, you continue travelling through
this poorly made tunnel. The fear of collapse is non-existent as you stare at
the defiant rocks that resisted the strength of ravaging waters, erosion and
orc-driven picks. It is equally obvious, that falling here wouldn't prevent
those same defiant rocks from piercing your body and leaving you dead for the
rodents to consume.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
Carved in a circular shape, the tunnel ahead of you looks quite unnatural. Off
to one side, huge piles of dirt lay compressed with time, lending evidence to
the suspicion that this tunnel was made by no miner's hands. Small tracks of
slime and the moist sticky walls around seem more like remnants left by a
burrowing creature. It seems evident that this tunnel was never part of any
original design.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
The smooth walls are broken here by larger stubborn rocks that refused to yield
to the passage of time. Though worn away by erosion in some distant age when
this area was flooded, they yet break the uniformity of the tunnel walls, giving
them a lumpy, sickly appearance. Passage to the east is blocked by a large
wooden door.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
The walls breathe a cool air, stored deep from with the mountain, upon your
face. The walls, slightly moist, continue smoothly to the South and West. Faint
cart tracks, etched into the stone floor from years of loaded ore carts, lead
you towards the quarries where the orcs mined much of their metals. Rusted
water, collected in the ruts, resemble red-blood arteries, guiding you to their
destiny.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
Like a godly barrier, the rock to the east withstood the tunnelling of The
Burrow, forcing it to angle north around it like a stream bending around a rock.
From where you stand you can barely glimpse the grey and dusky face of the
passage as it fades into blackness to the west. To the east, you can see no
alteration of its straight and rigid course, disappearing beyond your sight in a
sea of earthen haze.
name: The Burrow

desc:
Perhaps the rock had been simply too tough to break, or perhaps because of some
shuddering of the earth, whatever the origin, The Burrow, instead of continuing
straight on its east to west course bends to the south. To the east, you peer
along its length til it is lost in the veil of darkness that surrounds you on
all sides. To the west, The Burrow slides south, curving like the back of a
slithering serpent far beneath the skies and sun, under this dark and distant
peak.
name: The Burrow

desc:
Nearing the secret junction with Wolf's Gate, the pass continues east-west.
Here, the tunnel widens even more, pratically into an open cavern, allowing
troops to assemble without impeding swift travel along the pass. The smooth
granite walls are nicked and gnashed here, the victims of angry swords and shows
of bravado.
name: Goblins' Pass

desc:
You stand within one of the many miles of The Burrow, a huge tunnel which seems
to snake like a giant worm under the mountain. The walls have long been shorn to
a smooth, almost slick surface. The floor curves like the bottom of a bowl,
smooth and quick to carry you. To either side, to the east and to the west, The
Burrow continues its tunnelling, unaware of the time or seasons passing far
above, in the sky and heavens of Arda.
name: The Burrow

desc:
The walls ring with the clatter of metal and commotion. Bootsteps can be heard
clanging off the walls and the grumblings and murmurs of Orcs echo off the walls
to your ears. To the south, a bright fire casts light into this room, bringing
some heat to the cold smooth granite walls surrounding you.
name: A Cavern

desc:
This stuffy cave serves as an all purpose resting-eating-sleeping ground for the
Orc Guards. Piled in one corner, matted filthy furs lie, a small solace for the
weary orc. Heaped in another corner, piles of bones and rotting flesh, the meals
cast aside of busy or late guards.
name: A Cave

desc:
Dark and gloomy, even a brightly lit torch would fail to peel much darkness from
this blackly painted darkness. Cold draughts of air whose origins lie deep in
the mountain mix with icy water here and chill you to the bone. The wind whips
in an almost regular pattern, as though you are caught in the giant breaths of
the mountain.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
The walls bleed pasty red drops around you. Streaks of oxidized metal, turned
bright red, resemble violent scars upon these hollowed walls. Once perhaps, this
area was mined for precious metals. Now left in abandonment, the walls have
turned red with rust, giving an eerie and life-like appearance to this crumbling
tunnel.
name: A Cave

desc:
Like the hallways of a tossing ship, this tunnel swerves to the right and left,
pitching you forward and backwards. The rocks lay scarred here, bitten by the
picks and axes of a legion of miners, and yet defiantly intact. Standing upright
against the floor like a fanged snake, dangerously sharp rocks threaten to
stumble and impale the unwary traveller.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
Continuing along this narrow and scarred tunnel, it becomes clear that it is not
safe. Large polyps of huge stones bar you safe passage through most of the
tunnel, forcing you to constantly climb and shift from side to side. Eru only
knows why the miners felt a need to continue, but the passage continues to the
south and north.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
This irregularly shaped entrance is narrowed and made dangerous by large rocky
outcrops of unmeasurably hard and solid rock. Movement in here would be slow and
tedious, as one must carefully step to avoid stumbling. Here, the miners battled
with the very forces of Arda to tunnel a passageway, making it passable, but
barely.
name: A Tunnel Mouth

desc:
Three men abreast could span this tunnel carved out of the dark rock of this
mountain. One and a half men would it take to reach its roof. Standing within
its centre, in either direction, east or west, it continues into the gloom. The
curving walls arch above you oppressively, as if the entire weight of the
mountain rested upon the back of its smooth arch. As you pad along it, step
after step, as if you were stepping upon the coils of some long sleeping snake,
your steps echo softly in your ears. You stand within The Burrow.
name: The Burrow

desc:
As if the hand of Eru himself had dug deep into the mountain to twist this
underworldy road, The Burrow finds itself perverted from its straight course,
bent by some will to go where it now flows. To the west, it exerts its inertial
force. To the east, it is prevented, torn instead to the south, where it might
flow to the song of its making. Standing within The Burrow's embrace, you can
only wonder whether it was the hands of mortals that carved this place, or the
hands of gods.
name: The Burrow

desc:
As if the hand of Eru himself had dug deep into the mountain to twist this
underworldy road, The Burrow finds itself perverted from its straight course,
bent by some will to go where it now flows. To the east, it exerts its inertial
force. To the west, it is stayed, turning instead to the north, where it might
again fulfil the path chosen for it. From your standpoint, The Burrow flowing
around you, its walls hugging you like the coils of a snake, you wonder at the
simple marvel of its smooth hewn walls, its arched ceiling, and its
foot-flattened floor.
name: The Burrow

desc:
Here it is difficult to distinguish the black walls from the darkness around
you. Squinting for exits, you make out a shadowy silhouette to the east and
west, to the south, faint bootsteps and wailing can be heard.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
The granite walls around you make excellent sound conductors; voices and metal
boots can be heard stomping and clattering off the jagged walls ahead.
Attempting to discover where those sounds originate could prove a more difficult
task however. The jagged angled walls spin echos into random patterns and
directions, making them difficult to locate.
name: A Tunnel

desc:
Like a crack within the solid wall of stone, this small passage peters off into
the darkness. The walls are oppressively close and jagged and the floor is rough
and covered with rubble and dust. It is immediately apparent that, unlike The
Burrow, this small tunnel is very rarely used, if it is used at all. A few
broken weapons litter the floor, as well as strips of leather and splinters of
wood, but nothing at all of value.
name: A Tunnel Mouth

desc:
Like the home of some earth-wandering worm, the scope and the length of this
passage dwarfs you. It stretches to the east and west in its unfathomable path
through the mountain as if by the earth's will itself, this tunnel was made.
Whatever created this hollow bowl of air submerged in earth and rock is
anybody's guess. The orcs were known to have been skilled but this? The dwarves
were masters, but this? Is there some higher hand at work within this dark and
brooding mountain?
name: The Burrow

desc:
Opening into a wide mouth, this tunnel is smoothly crafted. The thick granite
walls hurl you along its wide domain safely and sure-footedly.
name: A Tunnel Mouth

desc:
Swirls of sulphur lie in veins along the black skin of these granite rocks. The
ancient river, unable or unwilling to head onwards, must have folded over on
itself, forcing the water behind it into a turbulent loop, and thus widening
this cavern. Immense by any standards, it would be a nice place to rest, if not
for the itching noxious sulphur hanging heavily in the air.
name: A Cave

desc:
Once perhaps a quiet pool of water, this craggy cave pokes sharply at your feet.
Made of razor-thin shale, walking on the ground is like walking on a roomful of
blades. Luckily, made of weak shale and limestone, they break underfoot, sending
up tiny plumes of fossilized dust and debris.
name: A Cave

desc:
Cold wet drops seep from the earthen walls here, immersing you to the waist in
deep slimy water. Deciding quickly that this probably is not the safest spot,
you grope for reassurance - what was that object that brushed against your leg?
Piercing your look into the water, you can determine nothing, lost in the
indomitable darkness around you.
name: A Pit

desc:
The corridor widens, and smoking torches obscure your vision. A series of iron
rings are embedded in the wall here, presumably to chain slaves until overseers
from deeper in the mines need them. Deeper into the mines you can hear the
sounds of digging, whereas to the west is only darkness and cobwebs.
name: At a Checkpoint

desc:
As the ore ran out along this path, the digging was abandoned. Now slag rock is
hauled in and stands in big piles waiting to be hauled away. A pale moth
flutters by you, headed towards the light you can see to the east.
name: Mineshaft

desc:
This tunnel ends abruptly at a pile of rubble and broken timber. Jutting out
from some stones you can see a dessicated thighbone. The beams overhead don't
look very stable, and occasionally bits of stone and dust fall from the ceiling.
name: Collapsed Tunnel

desc:
A sign hangs above a door to the north, written in a vain attempt at Westron.
The corridor branches off in several directions here and is beginning to get
confusing. Written in soot on the walls seem to be some sort of directions.
name: Mineshaft

desc:
The floor shows marks of heavy carts being brought through from the south. That
must be where the most heavy mining operations are now. From the west you can
hear a miserable moaning.
name: Mineshaft

desc:
The room is dominated by a central pit, in which large numbers of slaves are
kept. Some try to sleep on thin mats, while others stare blankly and try to
remember what the sky looks like. The smell coming from the pit is rank with
sweat, decay, and fear.
name: Slave Pits

desc:
This shaft has not been used in some time. A stake with a length of chain
attached is in the centre of the room. The sounds of the slave pits can be heard
to the east, while to the south are strange tracks and marks as if something had
been drug through the dust.
name: Mineshaft

desc:
A sharp bend leaves one standing in a cold underground stream about ankle deep.
Blind cave fish dart about in the water. Green algae line the rock, making it
slick to walk on. The stream seems to come from somewhere higher up in the caves
and is headed down towards the depths.
name: Underground Stream

desc:
The corridor tightens as it bends. The sound of running water comes from the
west. Velvety moss grows on the walls to the west, and the air in that direction
is more humid.
name: Sharp Bend

desc:
Moving through the quarry is quite treacherous. Open pits where the slaves dig
ore from the ground line the walkway, and fires burn continuously, illuminating
the high ceiling and making the shadows of stalactites dance. Bones are mixed in
with the rubble heaps, and the clamor of digging assaults the ears.
name: Midst of a Quarry

desc:
In the pits below are many slaves breaking rock and digging ore from the rock
walls. The ledge is narrow, and nothing is between you and a treacherous fall to
the quarry below. How the slaves get ore and rock up this ledge and back through
the corridors, is anybody's guess. The ledge slopes downward into the quarry to
the east.
name: Quarry Overlook

desc:
The mining has broken through a wall and into another cavern at a deeper level.
Looking down into the hole you can see where the orcs have placed wooden
ladders. Jagged stalagmites line the hole and would prove dangerous if one were
to slip. The clamour of the mines echoes down the corridor from the north.
name: Narrow Opening

desc:
Here, in typical orc fashion, refuse and garbage has been carelessly thrown.
Interspersed amongst the dirty cloth and piles of broken metal are decaying
corpses and rotting flesh. Hastily thrown dirt makes a minor covering for this
rotting cesspool, but the smell is still over-powering enough to roll your
stomach. This is where the orcs with no honor or dignity are left to spend their
eternal rest.
name: A Dark Pit

desc:
A high-domed ceiling curves up overhead, and the centre of the room is dominated
by a marble well. A carved swan sits on the edge and deep in the depths of the
well you can see water sparkling. Murals unseen by anyone for ages line the
walls.
name: Well of the Ancients

desc:
The feel of deep magic throbs in your head, filling your very soul with the
ancient magic of the Elves. Never has anything felt so peaceful as being
submerged in this cool blue water. Pain and exhaustion fade away as you dive
deeper into the heart of the well. Silence surrounds you, calming your heart and
soul.
name: Down a Well

desc:
Here at the bottom, small cave fish dart about in the rocks. Perhaps part of a
vast underground river, the water is cool and a current flows over you from out
of the darkness. Ancient stones line the bottom of the well, perhaps dropped
here by the Elves in a different age.
name: Well Bottom

desc:
Loose rock is all over the corridor, carried from the ore pits and quarries from
the east, up a sloping tunnel and into a great slag pit. The cold mountain winds
whistle here, howling eerily up and down the mineshafts.
name: Drafty Tunnel

desc:
An itching odour rises from the ground, soaked in rot and decay. Along with it a
thick mist spreads a few feet above the moss and pools of water like a blanket
cuddling the land. Between the isles of moss, muddy water runs to and fro in an
intricate pattern that runs miles at end in all directions. A peninsula in the
marsh stretches in from the north and drops sharply into the soaked land. A
branch of it divides the waters a bit further in the east.
name: Deep, Flowing Mire

desc:
Patches of grey moss and lichen float like isles in the shallow waters. An
occasional rotting tree springs up where the ground is firmer, but mostly the
landscape is flat and rolling, covered with moss and pools of water. At the
sides of the moss, small yellow flowers stick to the ground, avoiding the murky
waters below.
name: Undulating Marshlands

desc:
The River Gladden makes one of its last bends here, before it meets the large
Anduin, which then continues southbound. The surrounding landscape is mainly
constituted of marshes; however some remote, dark green spots may be forests,
which appear to be medium-sized.
name: A Bend in the Gladden

desc:
The mossy turfs slope away towards the Gladden River in the south. Although the
ground is sloping gently the pools of water have a firm stand in the earth, not
flowing in any direction. Close to the ground, a thick layer of mist lies as a
carpet over the waters and a stench of rot and decay fills the air. A sharp
incline in the marsh creates a steep streaming slope in the east.
name: Brink of the Gladden River

desc:
A sharp turn of the Gladden River cuts through the marshes here, dragging away
turfs of moss and lichen in its stream. The water roars as it passes and
stretches out again towards the south. The moss and mud, already sick with
water, are desperately holding a stand against the rushing water at the banks of
the current.
name: Along the Shores of the Gladden River

desc:
A prominent slope of the marsh reaches southwards into the Gladden River. Small
streams of flowing water stretch away, trying to reach the current in the south.
The heavy patches of moss peaking up from the shallow waters point out in all
directions, but in the south they come to an abrupt end in the streaming waters.
A firm scent of decay dominates the impression of this mire.
name: Descending Marshes

desc:
Dark muddy waters flow quietly between large patches of moss and lichen. Turfs
of grey hay stand wherever the ground is firm enough to support their fragile
roots. Between the straws, yellow flowers struggle on the soft ground. The whole
area is covered in a strange mist ascending from the ground, and with it a scent
of decay and rotten earth rises.
name: Grey Mosses

desc:
This incline in the marsh stands in sharp contrast to the surrounding mire. Here
green grass can grow freely as the soggy ground is turned into more manageable
soil. On the surrounding borders of the isle yellow flowers and high thick grass
grow in rows. Beyond the isle the dense mist reaches up from the moist ground.
It does not reach this hill, but stays around it like clouds around a mountain.
A snare of roots and water-soaked lichen makes the path to the east impossible
to pass.
name: Isle in the Marsh

desc:
From drenched moss and lichen this incline has ascended into a large island in
the marshes, dry in comparison with the surrounding mire. Flowers and green
grass hold to its firm soil with rigid determination. While the ground is much
drier than the surrounding marsh it is still drenched in water and walking on
the springy surface reveals water flowing beneath the mud. In the south branches
and grass, gathered by the barely visible streams, create a natural barrier.
name: Ascended Moss

desc:
In contrary with most other parts of these marshes, the water here does not lie
calmly in its pockets; it rather moves in a fluent motion into the south. The
quiet stream is fed by the countless pools of water in the north, and gains
speed as it moves ahead towards the Gladden River further away. The floor of the
mire is stirred by the moving water, and mud and dirt fills the current. To the
east a steep slope feeds more water from the marshes above.
name: Streaming Grey Water

desc:
Large isles of moss and wet soil come together and serve as a resting point for
travellers daring to venture into these lands. They also serve as growing ground
for the small type of yellow flowers, so common in these marshes. Between the
mossy isles dark pools of water cover the ancient lake floor.
name: Patches of Moss and Mud

desc:
Some middle-sized stones, most being covered by mosses and small algae on their
sides, brush the surface of the water, creating small eddies in this otherwise
calm and slow-flowing river. Some larger ones are visible to the west, probably
created in the river bend located there.
name: The Gladden River

desc:
This is the site of one of the last bends in the Gladden. Hereafter the river
heads straight east where it meets the great Anduin. It flows regularly on its
pebble-strewn riverbed, except around a lone huge rock that rises taller than a
man out of the water. It seems to have been stuck in the middle of the stream
for ages, something that is made evident by its smooth surface at water level.
name: End of a Bend in the Gladden

desc:
Here, the damp ground slopes gently downwards, as it dives under a bend in the
Gladden. South of here, the stream flows in from the west towards the east. A
few pebbles, hidden among the roots of the low and sparse vegetation, are
covered by wet mosses which cause the riverbank to be slippery and the travel
somewhat dangerous.
name: A Wet Riverbank

desc:
The Gladden makes a bend here in its path from the mountains, which is visible
to the north-west. This appears to be one of the last curves of the riverbed
before it meets the Anduin. While the northern riverbank forms into a gentle
slope, the southern riverbank has an abrupt wall of earth that stands close to
five feet tall in height.
name: A Slight Bend in the Gladden

desc:
The flow of the Gladden is slow here, steered between a gentle slope towards the
northwest and an earthen wall of medium height standing in both the south and
the east. Between the rare patches of weeds growing on the vertical surface,
some small holes can be distinguished.
name: A Bend in the Gladden

desc:
This flat slope is leading downwards to the Gladden river, which currently flows
from the north, then into the west. There is a heap of brick-sized stones, which
may have been aligned towards the north-west long ago, but the elapsed time and
the occasional water overflows dislocated them.
name: A Marshy Slope

desc:
This part of the marsh appears to look as though there is an outcropping of some
earth above the Gladden. It appears to border it with a bend in its flow, coming
from the west, then passing to the north. Directing itself to the east, it then
takes a last noticeable curve in its bend towards the south.
name: Marshes by a Bend in the Gladden

desc:
This is the last, straight part of the River Gladden; after its northern bend it
now flows slowly towards a last turn to the south. Its widening, muddy riverbed
is bordered on both sides by vast marshes, sometimes covered by some dark grass.
In spite of the material it flows on, the water is rather clear.
name: The Gladden River

desc:
This flat piece of land ends abruptly to the north, where there is a steep drop
down to the river. However, though most of the riverbank is flat, there are a
few collapsed areas of the soil, covered by some dark grass, which allows an
easy access to the river that flows several feet below.
name: A Flat Riverbank

desc:
While the surrounding landscape is flat, this part of the field here is slightly
higher than its surroundings. It would allow a clear view of the nearby marshes
if it was not buried in a forest of yellow flowers. To the north, the Gladden's
bank can be reached after a gentle, downward slope in which a few stones are
embedded.
name: Overlooking the Gladden

desc:
This part of the marshes forms a slight gentle slope, between the upper marshes
to the north and a little terrain depression southwards. Four stones have been
placed to form a little circle, where one could easily keep a campfire burning.
Some rotten branches have been piled up around these stones, perhaps some kind
of precarious night shelters months ago.
name: At an Old Campsite

desc:
This part of the marsh, slightly higher than the surrounding piece of the land,
is bordered to the east by the calm flow of the Gladden. There is a slight, but
noticeable slope that travels downwards to the south-west, where an oval shape
can be distinguished.
name: Along the Gladden

desc:
After a straight part to the north, the Gladden bends around a bushy riverbank,
located in the northeast, and finally meets the great Anduin river to the east.
The bed is now quite large and flat, with a rather thin water layer that covers
it, while some islets are emerging here and there.
name: The Last Bend in the Gladden

desc:
The soft sound of the two rivers that border this rocky part of the marshes,
then meet in the north-east, is to be heard here. The Gladden flows to the north
into the small meeting lake, while the resulting waters flow eastward, then
south from here.
name: Marshes Bordered by Two Rivers

desc:
This portion of the marsh is quite elevated here, abruptly cut off to the
north-east by the enlarging bed of the Gladden. Another natural border lies to
the south-east, where the Anduin river creates a natural border. The western
part of the marsh lowers itself slowly through a slope, towards a large pool
which is visible in the distance. The soil here is covered by some dense, but
mostly very low vegetation.
name: Marshes between a Pool and Two Rivers

desc:
Some event in the history of the marsh has made this place lower than its
surroundings. The area is on the same level as the two rivers, the Anduin and
the Gladden, which flow several miles to the north and the east. The bottom of
this depression is now filled up with water, creating the large, opaque pool
that lies here now.
name: A Dip in the Marshes

desc:
Sticking out of the low vegetation, and marking the landscape, stands an old,
dead and twisted tree. It may have been an oak, though it is hard to tell since
its bark has been torn away by the passing years. Nearby, one can spot some
water-filled holes, deeper and larger than usual, which seem to be the last
traces of similar elders.
name: Marsh at a Lonely Tree

desc:
The few, low patches of tangled rushes add a clear tone to the otherwise sombre
colour of this flat marshland. Some puddles are scattered behind them, where
there are many pebbles of varying sizes. Most of these pebbles have been covered
by a thin layer of dark, green moss.
name: A Flat Marsh

desc:
Some shrubs and low trees are growing here on this slightly damp patch of
ground. While some thick-stemmed rushes can be found here, the vegetation is not
quite dense enough to hinder travel. It is, however, thicker than the
surroundings, except southwards where it transforms into a wooded area.
name: A Bushy Marsh

desc:
There is a continuation of all the western vegetation here, leaving the area to
be covered by scattered bushes, slightly darker than the low weeds that are in
growth between them. This appears to form a basin between the higher marsh that
is northwards and the forest that is visible to the southwest.
name: Sparse Bushes in a Marsh

desc:
This gentle slope in the marshes connects the large pool that is visible in the
northern depression with the surrounding average marsh level. The surrounding
vegetation is mostly lower than the western one that comprises some very widely
scattered bushes, adding some height to the area.
name: A Gentle Slope

desc:
The marsh here is slightly higher than the surrounding area, that ranges from
the north to the southwest. A terrain depression appears to be lying in the
northwest, while the large Anduin can be noticed flowing slowly around here,
from the east to the south.
name: Marshes by a Bend in the Anduin

desc:
Creeping tangles of some bulrushes can slow travel in the northern parts of this
place. However, they are progressively replaced towards the south by a few, low
trees. Some thickets of small birches add a white touch to the otherwise darker
tones of this wooded marsh.
name: A Sparse Wood

desc:
This may be the border of what was once a cultivated field of flax plants long
ago, since the proportion of this plant among the weeds is higher than normal,
especially to the south. The former cultures are bordered by a forest towards
the south and the west.
name: Border of Some Ancient Fields

desc:
The low, but rather dense, vegetation contains a small part of useful plants.
Most of these are located in the neighbourhood of what appears to have been an
ancient field towards the southward. There are some vaguely aligned slabs along
an north-south axis which might be the remains of a trail.
name: Border of Ancient Fields

desc:
According to the amount of wild useful plants growing here, this part of the
marshes was once a cultivated field. Among others, flax plants seem to be the
most present in the west, but some cereals are also noticeable in the rest of
the field.
name: Humid Fields

desc:
Mixed weeds, medium shrubs and many flax plants have formed the vegetation of
this humid field. There is a relatively flat corner which cuts through into the
forest that is visible in both directions to the west and south. A small ruin
can be noticed through the first, southern trees.
name: Humid Fields

desc:
These low woods are the continuation of the relatively small, southern forest.
The trees here are fairly small, widely spaced out from one another and mixed
with various shrubs. While this woody area somewhat extends itself northwards,
the landscape to the east seems to be much clearer.
name: Some Low Woods

desc:
None of the surrounding trees seem to be of an exceptional size. The trees are
mostly beeches and dark oaks, but some birches somewhat lighten the tones that
are introduced by the latter. A thin layer of mixed twigs have nearly hid the
sight of the soft ground.
name: A Mixed Forest

desc:
The forest forms a little clearing, which surrounds a small ruin. The trees seem
to disappear northwards, however, leaving place to a flat landscape. Some
climbing weeds are growing along the walls of the ruin. The walls appear to be
in very poor condition, but they are still holding parts of the ruined roof up.
name: The Border of a Forest at a Small Ruin

desc:
The very dense trees darken this place greatly. None of these trees seem to be
very high, however. Maybe is it because of the unstable and damp ground, which
seemingly caused the tallest of the trees to fall, leaving some large cavities
in the flat forest soil.
name: A Thick Forest

desc:
While some wheat plants can be seen here and there, the main vegetation growing
in this area is some high grass, which seems to be smothering most of the weeds.
Some of them are, however, climbing upon the wall of the small ruin that lies to
the west.
name: Marshes beside a Small Ruin

desc:
There are no pieces of furniture standing on the floor in this room. The soil is
made of raw earth, except for a small path of broken slabs between its western
and eastern doors. While the roof is still complete, it seems ready to collapse
and bears a regular hole in its centre. The remains of a small campfire can be
noticed underneath it.
name: A Ruined Building

desc:
Formerly supported by walls in poor shape, the northern part of the roof has
fallen down, ruined by the years and a termite colony. The debris is not the
only thing lying on the ground, however, since cubic shapes are standing in each
corner of the room, next to what may have been chimneys.
name: A Partly Roofless Room

desc:
The thick forest, which appears to continue to the north and east, comes to an
end suddenly, leaving an area of flat and damp plains. The various trees that
have grown here are less dense than the rest of the trees in the forest, just
letting enough light pass through their foliage for some small bushes to grow
beneath them.
name: The Border of a Thick Forest

desc:
This moist cavity in the ground, which is about five feet deep, is filled by the
odour of decaying, large roots that are still stuck into the earth here. Judging
by the holes in the walls, some of them were violently pulled out, probably when
the large tree that once grew here fell.
name: Under a Fallen Tree

desc:
Some puddles can be noticed between the roots of the eastern trees, that seem to
connect with the river which flows in that direction. Towards the northwest, the
relief is a bit higher. The trees have grown very thick there, with their roots
containing some small holes between them.
name: A Damp Forest

desc:
The opaque, waist-high water here is almost totally covered with ferns and some
other vegetation, making it impossible to discern what might be found beneath
the surface. South of here, the ground appears to be decidedly firmer, whereas
the soggy reaches of the swamp continues east of here.
name: Gladden Fields

desc:
Treacherous to say the least, the ground here is far below the fern-covered
surface of the sluggishly moving swamp water. The swamp continues west and south
of here, whereas to the east, the pace of the water can be noticed picking up
speed as the main current of the river Anduin cuts through the landscape.
name: Gladden Fields

desc:
The forest is very dense from all of the numerous oaks that have grown here. A
few, lonely weeds are in a great struggle to grow, due to the ground that lies
beneath them being permanently shadowed. The lowest part of these tree trunks do
not bear any living branch, probably because there would not be enough light at
this level. Low dead branches, however, impede any mounted movement.
name: Deep in an Oak Forest

desc:
While taller trees block the view in the other directions, it is quite possible
to have a peek at the eastern Anduin. The load-bearing branches of this oak are
hopefully still thick enough to safely carry some weight, but going any further
would become dangerous.
name: A Tree-Top

desc:
This part of the forest is made of oaks for the most part, however, it appears
less dense than the trees that stand westwards. The ground is slightly higher
than the water level of the Anduin, which flows east of the forest here. The
ground here is not high enough to prevent the formation of puddles between the
roots of some of the trees though.
name: A Forest Bordered by the Anduin

desc:
The firm yet springy soil here is covered with huge clumps of an unusual sort of
grass: it is at least four feet high and sports a dark, dark hue of green. It is
quite obvious that the lushness of this field is due to the proximity of the
Gladden Fields north and east of here. Further east from here, the mighty river
Anduin flows steadily through the lands.
name: Riverside Fields

desc:
Cold all year around, these blackish waters at first seem to be standing still.
Yet, upon closer inspection, a swift movement in the water can be observed below
the water-lily covered surface. The Anduin, whose mighty flow can be seen east
from here, obviously barely bothers to stir these weed-infested swamps.
name: Gladden Fields

desc:
The characteristic giant leaves of water-lilies are nearly covering the somewhat
stagnant surface of these leech-infested marshlands. East and south, the main
current of the mighty Anduin speeds southwards on its way to the sea, whereas
the Gladden Fields continue northwards. West of here, the ground looks a lot
firmer, supporting a stand of thin-trunked, gnarly birch trees.
name: Gladden Fields

desc:
Far firmer than the swampy marsh east of here, this field is covered by several
species of lush grass and finger-sized ferns. A handful of gnarly birches also
grow here, eagerly drinking the plentiful water that saturates these somewhat
spongy fields.
name: Plains by the Anduin

desc:
Far from flat, this rugged riverside field features a number of jagged boulders.
These huge rocks appear to be the nesting spot for some of the local birdlife,
at least judging from the plentiful droppings on and around them. Looking east,
one can bask in the sight of the mighty Anduin rounding a corner, as it cuts a
path through the undulating grasslands and high-grown old forests of this part
of Arda.
name: Fields by the Anduin

desc:
Gently sloping down towards the Anduin river east of here, this grassy plain is
dotted with jagged rocks and some hardy juniper bushes. By the river's edge, a
pile of fist-sized rocks comprise something that could almost be described as a
natural jetty, poking out fifteen yards from the bank into the river. Downstream
from the jetty, the lack of current forms a haven of slower-flowing water.
name: Fields by the Anduin

desc:
Thick green, almost carpet-like growth covers this springy strip of turf by the
river Anduin. East of here, a small copse comprised of a handful of slender
saplings partly obscures the view of the river. The outskirts of a thicker
forest can be seen south from here.
name: Plains by the Anduin

desc:
Being amply provided with water by the neighbouring river Anduin, this mixed
forest is comprised of a number of different tree species. However, quite a few
of them appear to be afflicted by some kind of parasite that makes their trunks
look all scurfy and rough. The forest continues further south, whereas north of
here, a broken plain opens up.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
Dark, yet somehow not that imposing, this riverside forest sports a mix of old
firs, oaks and alder trees. Whereas the same type of vegetation appears to
continue north from here, the forest to the south seems both lighter and
younger. East, the mighty river Anduin can be glimpsed between the trunks of the
trees.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
This little triangle of wild grass is bordered by the Anduin to the west, after
a vertical bank by some trees to the north-east. Also noticed, are the remains
of a trail towards the south. Some vaguely rectangular and big stones can be
seen lying here and there, partly buried into the earth.
name: A Marshy Riverbank

desc:
Some scattered stones among many, wild weeds may be the remains of an ancient
trail. It seems to be coming from the south-east, meeting the Anduin River that
borders this little marsh to the west. There are large rocks that appear to form
an embankment of some sort.
name: Remains of a Trail by the Anduin

desc:
The Great Anduin River bends around this lonely hill, which is covered by some
wild grass. The eastern slope is bushier, as it sinks under the small forest
that can be seen to the east. Some stones with a regular shape are noticed near
the top of the hill, but their purpose remains unknown.
name: A Hill above the Anduin

desc:
The ancient trail, now in very poor shape, comes straight from the east, ending
in front of the Great Anduin River. The scattered slabs, among which wild weeds
grow, appear to continue in the water by huge stone blocks. The blocks may have
formed a pier for some small boats long ago, but they are now too dislocated to
be of any use.
name: End of an Ancient Trail

desc:
Comprised mostly of mid-aged pine and alder trees, this mixed forest partition
also sports a quite varied underbrush. One might think that this could be an
ideal playground for all kinds of wildlife. West and south from here, the forest
lightens up a bit. East of here, the forest opens up onto a huge plain.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
Here, amidst the normally peaceful, riverside forest, an untidy, semi-permanent
camp has been set up. Dirty, rotting pieces of canvas has been tied up between
some tree trunks and boulders, providing an effective but smelly wind shelter.
On the ground, more of the smelly, old pieces of canvas have been tossed out,
and are now being used as mattresses. Branches cut from pine trees serve as
stuffing and pillows. Leftover foodstuffs of unknown kinds and descriptions have
been thrown about, and whoever set this camp up does not appear to care whether
their camp site is tidy or not. The whole, grotty camp is centred around a
crudely set up hearth. On the outskirts, further signs of defilement are
present, as whoever camps here obviously takes pleasure in breaking every fresh
tree-branch and trampling every young sapling.
name: Rough Camp by the Anduin

desc:
Centred around a huge, dying oak tree, this light but solid forest belt grows
here surrounded by open areas both west and east of here. For some reason, the
trees and bushes appear as though they want to give the old oak all the room it
wants or needs, because there is a large, glen-like space that lies between the
old behemoth and the next ring of younger trees.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
The vegetation is very scant here, probably because the Anduin at some point
washed up a huge number of black, hard rocks and pebbles. These rocks litter
this riverside plain, and only a few strands of ferns grow between them. North,
east and south from here, a mixed forest grows about, whereas the view west
overlooks the Anduin and the swampy areas beyond.
name: Rough Plains by the Anduin

desc:
Rough and uneven, the ground here on this riverside field is full of black rock
shards and jagged boulders. The field is only sparsely graced with a few, tough
bushes and patches of green grass. South and east of here stands a forest
comprised of what appears to be truly majestic old oak trees. The rugged fields
appear to continue north from here.
name: Rough Plains by the Anduin

desc:
A family of majestic old oaks make out the core of this otherwise mixed and
heterogeneous forest. A cluster of pesky and young birches seem to be the most
recent upstarts. At ground level, the forest floor is covered by tough but hardy
vegetation. Below the foliage, the ground is made up by various debris and
pine-needles.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
A mix of old to middle-aged oaks and several tall and straight firs make up the
trees of this untouched forest. The forest floor is almost devoid of any
undergrowth. East of here, a vast and undulating plain appears to open up, while
the forest continues in all the other cardinal directions.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
A few, majestic old oaks have in a most persuasive manner claimed this part of
the forest as their domain, overshadowing the other trees by any measure. The
undergrowth here is comprised by the usual plethora of grasses, mosses and
bushes. West from this place, the waters of a great river can be glimpsed in
between the tree-trunks.
name: Great Oaks by the Anduin

desc:
A large number of boulders, covered by six-inch thick, green mosses and lush
patches of evergreen grass comprise the underbrush in this part of the forest.
Mostly tall, mature, conifers grow here, but here and there one can spot other
types of trees that have found a spot to grow and prosper upon.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
Pointing straight to the sky, these silvery-green firs must be supreme material
for ship's masts or flag-poles. Among the trees, moss covered rocks and boulders
are embedded in the underbrush. East and south, the forest opens up onto what
looks like an undulating, grassy plain. The forest continues north and west.
name: Tall Firs

desc:
Some animal must have had a field day here, trying to dig up something that was
far below the ground; there can be no other explanation for the three foot deep
pit that has been hastily dug here in the middle of the wilderness. North of
here, a looming and tall forest comprised by tall and proud fir trees can be
seen, whereas the rolling grasslands so prevalent in this part of Arda reaches
out in the other directions.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
Assessing this plain, one could easily come to the conclusion that a few trees
have decided to escape the dense forest clump that stands north of here.
However, upon further inspection, one can discern that quite a few trees that
had grown in between have been chopped down. If one was to guess, one would
probably be led to believe that it took place quite some time ago, since the
chopped-off tree stumps have already turned into a silver-grey hue.
name: Plains South of a Forest

desc:
Not far away from the great river Anduin, this field is quite fertile and
provides plenty of nourishment for most kinds of herbivores: thick, lush grasses
and the occasional birch tree litter the landscape. A few rocks and boulders
provide cover for small animals to hide from each other or from whatever threats
that might be found on this idyllic little patch.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
A few, moss-covered stones and a cluster of lowly juniper bushes are the only
features of this plain that offset the monotony of an unbroken horizon. Far to
the east, however, one can barely discern some kind of shadow, a dark line above
that horizon that could possibly be a cliff wall of some sort. Onwards travel is
possible in all the cardinal directions.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
Gently sloping eastwards, these green and undulating fields make quite a decent
home for a number of small animals. Various kinds of shrubs and bushes, along
with a few young oaken saplings, comprise the flora of this place. South-west of
here, the Anduin river can be noticed through all of the shrubbery; the mighty
flow appears to be rounding a corner, twisting away on its steady southward
journey.
name: Undulating Grasslands

desc:
This slight elevation in the rolling grasslands overlooks a sweeping bend in the
mighty river Anduin. The river, which carves a route from north to east, is
certainly far too broad to be crossed without proper caution and equipment. The
view allows a vague glimpse of the forest across the river, as well as of the
rolling grasslands east of here. A handful of pebbles have been piled upon each
other here, resembling some kind of a marker.
name: Plains by the Anduin

desc:
Overlooking the mighty river Anduin, which is making its way through the
landscape west of here, this green and grassy field gently slopes down towards
the river. To the east of this patch of land, a forlorn wilderness reaches out
towards a faraway ridge at the horizon.
name: Plains by the Anduin

desc:
Tough, yet lush, these plains east of the mighty river Anduin could very well be
ideally suited for farming. However, there are no traces of permanent settlement
visible anywhere, just the predominant mix of grasses, shrubbery and bushes.
Quite a few telltale signs of wildlife are also present, and one might guess
that this place is a favourite trail for local animals to approach the river for
a refreshing drink.
name: Plains by the Anduin

desc:
This is a strip of land that juts out into a bend of the mighty Anduin. The
ground is littered with mossy boulders that are resting on a spongy bed of
green, waist-high ferns. To the south-east, a great wild plain opens up, whereas
east of here, a dark forest lurks on the horizon.
name: Plains by the Anduin

desc:
In contrast to the greenery surrounding this spot, this unusual field is quite
barren. Some sort of natural pollution must have taken place here at some point,
because the ground is covered with some sort of saline crust, mixed with a
fibrous, white fluff. The only things thriving here are a few thick old bushes.
name: Barren Plain

desc:
This is another bend in the Anduin, similar to those that can be seen in the
south-west and the north-east. However, the riverbank is more varied, with a
grass-covered hill to the south. There is a small forest noticeable towards the
southeast, while the eastern part is covered by a bushy marsh.
name: A Small Bend in the Anduin

desc:
What might have been some fields, once long ago, can be seen on the western side
of the riverbank. However, these few, useful plants are largely intermixed with
weeds and some high grass. On the other side of this wide part of the Anduin, a
grassy hill can be noticed.
name: The Anduin by Some Ancient Fields

desc:
To the east of this relatively slow and wide part of the Anduin, what appears to
be the remains of an old trail, can be distinguished under the wild grass that
grows here. A small ruin, partly covered by wild weeds, can be distinguished to
the west behind a marshy riverbank.
name: The Anduin River

desc:
The Anduin still flows quite slowly in its wide bed here, while it tightens and
speeds up as it travels into the south. The roots of the trees that grow on the
western bank maintain their consolidating grip only on a part of the humid and
unstable soil, causing the riverbank to be quite irregular after ages of water
erosion.
name: The Anduin along a Forest

desc:
The Anduin river, that flows from the north, bends around a tree-covered bank
that can be noticed southwards, while its width decreases gradually, speeding up
its flow. The remains of a small trail, covered by weeds, is noticed on the
eastern bank.
name: A Bend in the Anduin

desc:
Taking its time on its route to its ultimate destination, the sea, the flow of
the mighty river lends its waters to the tranquil marshlands west of here. On
the eastern shore, a rough and jagged plain serves as the receiving buffer for
spring-floods long passed. Looking closer, one can see mounds of whitening
debris that has piled up there over the years.
name: The Anduin

desc:
Unusually sluggish for being such a swift and mighty river, the Anduin seems to
be pausing for breath here, widening and delegating its waters into a great
marshy area west of here. To the east, a mixed patch of forest is overlooking
the passage of the river. Gazing north, the river appears to be less broad, and
the flow of the water seems faster.
name: The Anduin

desc:
The Anduin is slightly picking up pace here, but lots of water is being diverted
into the marshes west of here. Eastward, a rough and desolate field is gathering
debris from spring floods passed, whereas the river approaches a bend further
downstream. Gazing west, the peaks of the snow-capped Misty Mountains rise above
the forest-lined horizon.
name: The Anduin

desc:
Approaching a great bend, the mighty Anduin is gaining in pace as it makes its
way downstream, all the while creating eddies of all sizes. East of here, a
stand of ancient oaks loom above an otherwise mixed forest flora. Upstream, the
river looks decidedly slower and quite a lot broader, as its waters appear to be
diverted onto some kind of marshlands.
name: Bend in the Mighty Anduin

desc:
Broad and mighty, the great river Anduin cuts through a lush but uninhabited
plain. The main current of the river stays close to the centre, whereas closer
to the shores, the pace of the murky waterway is decidedly slower. Debris from
previous spring-floods has piled up on both shores. Downstream, the river heads
steadily in a southern direction, whereas there appears to be a easterly bend
further up north.
name: The Anduin

desc:
Steady on its southern course, the broad and mighty river Anduin makes good
headway through these forlorn wilderness plains. On the western shore grows a
stand of young alder trees, trees that are partly obscuring the view of what
lies beyond. To the east, a rugged plain develops towards the horizon.
name: The Anduin

desc:
Keeping a high and steady pace, the main current of the great river ploughs
through the land in a north-south direction, a feature of the land that has
effectively divided these lands into two separate partitions. West of here a
forest grows close to the river's edge. On the eastern shore, a green and grassy
field rolls gently towards the horizon.
name: The Anduin

desc:
Steady and fast, the main current of the river keeps itself to the centre part
of the broad waters of the Anduin. At the shores, froth whipped up by eddies and
currents has gathered in fluffy piles among the shore-side debris. West of the
river grows a tall, mixed forest. To the east, a rolling grassland stretches
out, dotted here and there by bushes and lonely trees.
name: The Anduin

desc:
The Anduin heads into the southwest, its bed forming a rough line, broken apart
in many places by slight bends among the usually flat marshes. Some floating
water plants and algae can be noticed here and there, bordering these widely
spaced out and grassy riverbanks.
name: A Small Bend in the Anduin

desc:
The Anduin makes a bend here in its bed around a hill, being located towards the
south-east. It is bordered to both the north and west by vast marshes. While the
river seems to make another bend not far to the east, its course appears to be
roughly straight to the south.
name: A Bend in the Anduin

desc:
The Anduin river, which flows from the north-east, progressively tightens its
bed towards the south. Here, it leaves the slow Gladden Fields and returns to
its normal, fast flowing stream as it travels. Some old leaves can be seen in
some of the small inlets, leftovers of the last autumn.
name: The Anduin Surrounded by Trees

desc:
Curving west to south, this section of the river is a part of a larger s-shaped
bend in the Anduin. North from here, a wet and broad swamp feeds debris into the
river's main current. Passage westwards onto a rugged, boulder-strewn plain is
also possible. Downstream, eddies and maelstroms speak of a current that is
increasing its pace as it makes its way through these forsaken lands.
name: Bend in the Mighty Anduin

desc:
The water in the marshy lake is dark and cold. Mud and mossy debris fills every
inch of the pool, and no sunlight reaches from the surface above. Along the
bottom moss and lichen resides, preventing deeper venturing, but the moss itself
must reach even further down into the ground.
name: Beneath the Dark Surface

desc:
Painted images line the walls, though many are blurred with age and even more
have been defaced by the orcs. This is a massive corridor which runs north and
south, but the mining operations above have caused a cave-in to the north. To
the south is an arch, leading into a hall.
name: Hall of Murals

desc:
The orcs have created an ingenious machine for crushing rock. A pulley and gear
system hooked up to some huge metal teeth crushes rock as it is fed into the
machine by slaves. More weary slaves work at turning a turnstile to grind the
rock into dust. Judging from some bloody pulp you see mixed in with the dust,
the overseers enjoyed testing out their machine.
name: Ore Pit

desc:
A desk, propped up at one end by a couple of elf skulls, marks the centre of the
room. A beaten old chair made of genuine warghide is the room's only other
furniture. A handful of rocks, a severed humanoid hand, and a heavy club serve
as paperweights. The foreman is obviously an orc who spends most of his time out
of the office. A huge box labeled 'IN' contains a handful of elf ears. The
office looks to have been abandoned for a while.
name: Abandoned Foreman's Office

desc:
Small stones cover the bottom of part of the slow-flowing Gladden River. Most of
them are covered by a slippery layer of dark green moss. There is a bend in the
bed of the river that is noticed to the west, soon followed by another one which
is downstream towards the east.
name: Ford on the Gladden River

desc:
The tunnel ends abruptly in a huge web, with several cocoons visible from where
you are. Large amounts of bones are scattered about on the floor, and the smell
of rotting flesh is almost overwhelming.
name: Cobwebbed Tunnel
